questions.question: Define cloud computing according to NIST and list its five essential characteristics.
keyPoints: NIST definition: on-demand network access to shared pool of configurable computing resources
Five characteristics: on-demand self-service, broad network access, resource pooling, rapid elasticity, measured service

questions.question: Explain the three cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) and the security responsibilities of providers versus customers in each.
keyPoints: IaaS: Provider handles physical infrastructure; Customer handles OS, apps, data, network
PaaS: Provider handles infrastructure + OS + runtime; Customer handles apps and data
SaaS: Provider handles almost everything; Customer handles access management and data classification

questions.question: What are the major security challenges faced in cloud computing environments?
keyPoints: Data breaches and data loss
Account hijacking and insecure APIs
Insider threats and malicious insiders
Malware injection and ransomware
Lack of cloud security expertise
Multi-jurisdictional compliance issues
Multi-tenancy isolation vulnerabilities

questions.question: Discuss the concept of multi-tenancy in cloud computing and the security implications associated with it.
keyPoints: Multi-tenancy: multiple customers share same physical infrastructure
Resource isolation is critical for security
Risks: VM escape attacks, side-channel attacks, data leakage through misconfigurations
Mitigation: proper virtualization, strong access controls, regular security audits

questions.question: What is a Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB)? Explain its functions in cloud security.
keyPoints: CASB is security software between cloud users and providers
Functions: visibility into cloud usage, data security enforcement, threat protection, compliance management
Monitors user activities and detects anomalous behavior
Enforces security policies for shadow IT

questions.question: Explain Identity and Access Management (IAM) in cloud environments and discuss common IAM mechanisms.
keyPoints: IAM manages who can access what resources in cloud
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds extra security layer
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) assigns permissions by job function
Federated identity allows single sign-on across domains
Principle of least privilege should be applied

questions.question: What are the different cloud deployment models? Explain each with appropriate use cases.
keyPoints: Public Cloud: services over internet, for general workloads, cost-effective
Private Cloud: dedicated to one organization, for sensitive data and strict compliance
Hybrid Cloud: combination, for sensitive data on-premise + scalable public workloads
Community Cloud: shared by organizations with common concerns, for regulated industries

questions.question: Explain the Shared Responsibility Model in cloud security. How do security responsibilities differ between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS? Give examples of what each party is responsible for in each model.
keyPoints: Shared responsibility divides security between CSP and customer
IaaS: Provider secures physical, network, hypervisor; Customer secures OS, apps, data, configs
PaaS: Provider adds OS and runtime; Customer secures apps, data, access
SaaS: Provider secures almost everything; Customer secures user access and data
Examples: In IaaS customer manages firewall rules; In SaaS provider manages application security
Emphasize: more managed service = less customer security responsibility

questions.question: Discuss the various security approaches and tools used in cloud environments. Include discussion of encryption, CASB, CSPM, and IAM mechanisms.
keyPoints: Encryption: at rest and in transit, key management strategies
CASB: visibility, data security, threat protection, compliance
CSPM: continuous monitoring, misconfiguration detection, compliance automation
IAM: MFA, RBAC, federated identity, single sign-on
Microsegmentation: network isolation within cloud
Zero Trust architecture: never trust, always verify

questions.question: What is virtualization security in cloud computing? Discuss the security concerns associated with hypervisors and explain measures to secure virtualized environments.
keyPoints: Hypervisor security is critical as it manages all VMs
Risks: VM escape, hypervisor vulnerabilities, VM sprawl, side-channel attacks
Security measures: regular patching, VM isolation, access controls, monitoring
VM escape prevention: proper configuration, vulnerability management
Virtual machine sprawl management: tracking and controlling VM deployment
Hypervisor hardening: minimal attack surface, secure configurations

questions.question: Explain the concept of Security as a Service (SECaaS). List and describe the different security services that can be offered through this model.
keyPoints: SECaaS: outsourcing security functions to specialized third-party providers

Services include: Identity and access management, Vulnerability scanning, Intrusion detection and prevention, Data loss prevention, Security information and event management (SIEM), Email security, Web application firewall
Benefits: cost-effective, expertise access, scalability, 24/7 monitoring
Common providers: McAfee, Symantec, Palo Alto Networks, Fortinet
